How we help › Pain & Injury Support
For all • All ages • Every stage of life

Support to understand your pain, and a plan to ease it

Whether it started suddenly or has been building for years, we take the time to find out what is actually going on, and build a plan to help you feel and move better.

Call 07773 814488
What it's for · plain outcomes, no sub modality names, per the services messaging map · sidebar keeps the booking route visible
What it's for

For pain that hasn't been properly understood

Pain & Injury Support is for anyone dealing with a new injury, a flare up, or pain that has been around for longer than it should, regardless of age or ability. Some people arrive after an accident or a sudden setback; others have lived with a problem for months or years, tried a few things, and never quite got to the bottom of it. Whatever brought you here, the aim is the same: understand what is actually driving the pain, not just where it is felt, and help you move and feel better.

Every visit starts with an in-depth assessment, so treatment is built around your problem rather than a standard routine. As things improve, the plan is adjusted with you, whether that means easing an acute injury, managing a long standing condition, or helping you feel confident in your body again.

Old injuries can leave their mark. As the body heals it lays down scar tissue, and where that tissue binds to what surrounds it, it can leave an area that feels tight, moves less freely than it should, or feels tender, numb or simply different to the touch. It is one reason pain sometimes outlasts the injury that caused it. Where old scarring and adhesions are part of your picture, the assessment will find them, and the plan works to ease the restriction and help the area move and feel more like itself again.

Get in touch

The easiest first step is a conversation. Tell us what is going on and we will point you the right way, with no pressure at all.

07773 814488
Three benefit tiles · standard ivory tiles now the veil below carries the rhythm break
What to expect

What Pain & Injury Support involves

An in-depth assessment

We take the time to understand what is actually driving your pain, not just where you feel it.

Hands-on care

Skilled, targeted care to ease pain, reduce swelling and support your body's own healing.

A plan that adapts with you

Ongoing support and guidance, adjusted as your pain and injury change, so progress keeps moving forward.

Veiled photo band · 70% navy wash, ported from the homepage CTA treatment · photo SportsClinical-113, supplied by Dean What it means for you

Care that follows the pain to its source

Pain rarely stays where it started. Treatment looks at how the whole area is working together, not just the spot that hurts, so the plan addresses what is actually driving it.

Reassurance · approved copy, reused verbatim from Our Approach · safe, gentle, at your pace Reassurance

Safe, gentle, and at your pace

Everything is done with care and at a pace that suits you, and nothing is rushed or forced. The aim throughout is simple: that you feel in good hands, understood, and steadily better.

Recognised and accountable

Held to recognised professional standards

Members and registrants of the Royal Society of Medicine, the Complementary and Natural Healthcare Council, the Institute for Functional Medicine, the Society of Sports Therapists, the Institute of Sport and Remedial Massage and the British Medical Acupuncture Society, among others.
